{"key_event":"Mexican-American War and territorial expansion","reasoning":"Civil society became denser and more mobilized through mass parties, reform movements, abolitionism, women’s-rights organizing, immigration-based associations, and settler networks, though slavery, nativism, and Indigenous dispossession limited whose society was empowered. State power rose with successful war-making, diplomacy, annexation, territorial governance, and coercive expansion, but remained bounded by party competition, localism, fiscal limits, and deepening sectional conflict.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":7.8,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":6.7}
